# JOS-S45 — Contract Doc-Standard

## Purpose

Lock the shape of a Contract artifact. Every JOS Contract conforms.

## Boundary test (before authoring)

Per JOS-C46 + JOS-D0083, an artifact is a Contract ONLY IF:

- It is a Spec narrowed to **mechanically verifiable** invariants.
- It is a **cross-party** invariant (producer + consumer both depend on it).
- Breaking it causes **concrete downstream failure**, not just inconvenience.

If interpretation is needed → Spec.
If single-sided shape → Spec.
If quality criteria → Standard.

## Author voice

- **Binding language.** "X MUST Y." Per RFC-2119 style.
- **Schema explicit.** Every Contract references a Spec it derives from.
- **Mechanical test named.** Each invariant has a check that returns true/false.

## Required frontmatter

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id: "JOS-K##"` | YES | sequential |
| `uuid: "<v4>"` | YES | |
| `title: "<Short Contract Title>"` | YES | |
| `type: contract` | YES | |
| `owner: <name>` | YES | |
| `solve_for: > <paragraph>` | YES | |
| `stage_label:` | YES | |
| `flexibility: required` | YES | Contracts are binding by definition |
| `domain:` | YES | |
| `provenance_tier:` | YES | |
| Universal cascade fields | YES | |
| `derives_from:` (composition, per JOS-C51) | REQUIRED | `{specs: [...]}` — the Spec this Contract narrows |
| `guarantees:` (composition, per JOS-C51) | REQUIRED | `{capabilities: [...]}` — what this Contract enables downstream |
| `references:` | OPTIONAL | |

## Required sections (body)

1. **The promise** — one-sentence statement of the binding invariant.
2. **Mechanical test** — exactly how to verify compliance (script, gate, etc.).
3. **Parties bound** — who must honor this contract (producer + consumer).
4. **Breakage consequences** — what fails when the contract breaks.
5. **Connected Objects** — Spec it derives from + Capability it guarantees + Gate that enforces.

Optional sections: worked examples, migration notes for version changes.

## Body length guidance

- **Typical**: 80-300 lines (1500-4500 chars)
- **Floor**: ~60 lines — Contracts can be tight
- **Ceiling**: ~500 lines

## Composition (per JOS-C51)

Contracts declare two outbound verbs:
```yaml
derives_from:
  specs: [JOS-SPEC-##]
guarantees:
  capabilities: [JOS-CAP-##]
```

Per JOS Dog Food audit, 6 of 8 current Contracts have NO references. Phase 3.2 backfill required.

## Anti-patterns

- **Contract without `derives_from:`.** Floats free from any Spec.
- **Contract without mechanical test.** That's a Standard.
- **Single-party Contract.** If only one side cares, it's a Spec.
- **Contract that's actually a Rule.** Rules are JOS-internal binary tests; Contracts are cross-party promises.

## Worked examples

- **JOS-K01 Frontmatter Schema Contract** — exemplar: derives from SPEC-007, enforced by GATE-001.

## Validation

Forthcoming Gate: every Contract has typed `derives_from.specs` (≥1) + `guarantees.capabilities` (≥1) + the 4 required body sections.

- **[JOS-D0083]** Spec vs Contract upgrade criterion
